Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the detailed evidence gathered, the anterior two-thirds of the tongue are primarily supplied by the lingual nerve, which is a branch of the mandibular division of the trigeminal nerve (V3). Several references confirm this fact explicitly and highlight how the lingual nerve provides somatic sensation to this portion of the tongue, contradicting any suggestion that another nerve might serve this region predominately. Therefore, I reiterate agreement with our initial conclusion.
